name: executive_en
description: Decision-oriented executive voice for briefings, memos, and board-level summaries. Short, direct sentences, no hedging, action-ready conclusions. Designed for time-constrained senior audiences.

sentence_length.notes: Short, declarative sentences. One idea per sentence. No subordinate clause stacking. Bullets preferred for parallel items.

recommendation_pattern: Direct, named-owner recommendations: '[Owner] should [action] by [date/condition].' No hedging. Explicit tradeoffs in parentheses if needed.
number_embedding: Numbers lead, context follows. Example: '23% improvement in 6 months (Smith et al. 2024). This exceeds the 15% target. Caution: sample size was small.'
term_introduction: Technical terms avoided or replaced. If unavoidable, one-word gloss in parentheses. Example: 'SPECTER2 (AI relevance scoring) ranks papers automatically.'
tone: Confident, direct, action-oriented. No academic hedging. No emotional language. Every sentence earns its place. If uncertain, say so explicitly in one sentence.
uncertainty_language: Data is limited. Treat as preliminary.
This is an estimate based on incomplete data.
Expert consensus is split on this point.
dos: Lead with the conclusion, not the reasoning
Use bullet points for parallel options or evidence
Cut every word that does not add information
Use bold for critical terms or decisions
donts: No academic hedging (may, suggests, appears to) for known facts
No footnotes in the main document — use an appendix

structural_patterns.opening: Lead with the decision or finding: 'This briefing addresses [problem]. Key finding: [X]. Recommended action: [Y].'
structural_patterns.body: Problem -> Evidence (quantified) -> Options (2-3 max) -> Recommendation. Each section under one page.
structural_patterns.conclusion: Single paragraph: restate recommendation, owner, timeline, and next review date.

paragraph_length.notes: Short paragraphs. White space is a feature, not waste. Long blocks signal buried key information.

evidence_style: Cite source + year + single key metric. Example: 'Smith et al. (2024): 23% uplift, n=500.' Avoid quoting methodology in the main text.
audience_targeting: C-suite, board members, senior policy advisors. Assumes high intelligence, very limited time. Domain knowledge varies — avoid field-specific terms.
